name: Lofoten Fjord
notes: Cold, deep, dramatic saltwater fjord beneath the peaks. Drop heavy jigs into the dark water for cod and the occasional halibut the size of a door.

species.name: Atlantic Cod
species.behavior: Schools over deep reefs, takes jigs near the bottom.
species.realInfo: Winter 'skrei' cod migrate to Lofoten to spawn — the famous season.

species.name: Atlantic Mackerel
species.behavior: Summer shoals that hit shiny feathers at low light.

species.name: Atlantic Halibut
species.behavior: A barn door on the seabed. A genuine endurance war.
species.realInfo: Halibut can exceed 2m; landing a big one can take an hour.

species.name: Kraken's Lure
species.behavior: Something ancient let go of this. You shouldn't have it.
species.realInfo: Norse sailors feared the kraken in these very waters.

strategy: Drop heavy jigs straight down — the fish hold deep along the fjord walls.
Let the jig hit bottom, then work it up; cod and halibut feed near the floor.
Bundle up. The cold is part of the fight.
